claimant_lives_in_uk.description: Whether the claimant lives in the UK.

children.description: A list of children the claimant is claiming for.

lives_with_claimant.description: Whether the child lives with the claimant.

in_approved_education.description: Whether the child is in approved education or training (e.g. A-levels, GCSEs, NVQ up to level 3). Only relevant for children aged 16-19.

in_extension_period.description: Whether the child (aged 16 or 17) has left education and registered with a government-sponsored careers service or the armed forces (qualifying for a 20-week extension).

upkeep_per_week.description: The amount in £ per week the claimant contributes towards the child's upkeep (food, clothes, pocket money, etc.). Only relevant if the child does not live with the claimant.

another_claimant_has_priority.description: Whether another person who also lives with the child has priority for claiming Child Benefit.

another_claimant_lives_with_child.description: Whether someone who lives with the child is already claiming Child Benefit for them.

care_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in local authority care.

care_home_24h_per_week.description: Whether the child spends at least 24 hours per week at home while in local authority care.

hospital_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in hospital or residential accommodation.

claimant_spends_on_child.description: Whether the claimant regularly spends money on the child (e.g. food, clothes, books) while the child is in hospital or residential accommodation.

is_fostered.description: Whether the child is fostered by the claimant.

council_pays_for_child.description: Whether the local council pays towards the child's accommodation or maintenance.

works_24_plus_hours.description: Whether the child works 24 or more hours per week in paid employment.

started_apprenticeship_in_england.description: Whether the child has started an apprenticeship in England.

receives_qualifying_benefits.description: Whether the child receives qualifying benefits in their own right, such as Universal Credit or Employment and Support Allowance.

test_case.agent_script:
=== YOUR SITUATION PROFILE ===
You live in the UK.
You are inquiring about your 1 child:
  - Alex is 6 years old and lives with you.

Here are the exact details of your circumstances to use when answering the agent's questions:

Regarding Alex:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 6 (under 16)
  - Child lives with claimant
  - Child in care for 7 weeks (within 8-week limit)
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

expected_eligibility.name: Alex
expected_eligibility.reason: Claimant lives in the UK; Child is 6 (under 16); Child lives with claimant; Child in care for 7 weeks (within 8-week limit); Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation; Child is not fostered; Child does not work 24+ hours/week; Child has not started an apprenticeship in England; Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right
circumstances: Child in care for 7 weeks (within 8-week limit)
Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Hello! I'd be happy to help you find out if you're eligible for Child Benefit.

To assess your eligibility, I'll need to ask you a few questions about your circumstances.

Let's start with the basics:

**How many children would you like to claim Child Benefit for, and what are their names and ages?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Hello! Thanks for your help. I'm looking to claim for my child Alex, who is 6 years old.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Hello! Thank you for getting in touch. I'd be happy to help you determine if you're eligible for Child Benefit for Alex.

Let me ask you a few questions to assess your eligibility.

**Do you live in the UK?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, I live in the UK.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Great, thank you for confirming that.

**Do you live with Alex, or if not, do you contribute at least the same amount as Child Benefit (currently £25.60 per week for the eldest child) towards looking after them — for example, on food, clothes, or pocket money?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, Alex lives with me at home.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Lovely, thank you for that information.

Just a couple more questions to make sure we've covered everything:

**Is Alex currently in hospital, care, or residential accommodation provided by the local council?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Well, Alex has been in care for a little while - about 7 weeks or so - but they're not there full-time.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for sharing that. To help me understand Alex's situation better:

**Does Alex spend at least 24 hours a week at home with you?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Actually, I need to correct myself - Alex has been in care for those 7 weeks, but during that time they weren't coming home regularly. They're back with me now though.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for clarifying that. I'm glad to hear Alex is back home with you now.

Since Alex has been in care for about 7 weeks (which is under the 8-week limit) and is now back living with you, that's helpful to know.

Just one more question to confirm:

**Is anyone else currently claiming Child Benefit for Alex?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, nobody else is claiming Child Benefit for Alex.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Thank you for confirming that. I now have all the information I need to assess your eligibility for Child Benefit for Alex.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Based on the information you've provided, I'm pleased to let you know that **you appear to be eligible for Child Benefit for Alex**.

Here's a summary of why:

- ✅ You live in the UK
- ✅ Alex is under 16 years old (6 years old)
- ✅ Alex lives with you, so you are responsible for them
- ✅ Although Alex was in care for about 7 weeks, this is under the 8-week limit that would normally affect your payments, and Alex is now back home with you
- ✅ No one else is claiming Child Benefit for Alex

You can proceed to make a claim for Child Benefit through the official government channels. If you have any more questions or your circumstances change, feel free to ask!

reasonings: Alex is 6 years old, which is under 16, meeting the age requirement. The claimant lives in the UK and Alex now lives with them at home, meaning they are responsible for the child. Although Alex was in care for approximately 7 weeks, this is under the 8-week limit after which Child Benefit payments would normally be affected. Additionally, Alex is now back home with the claimant. No one else is currently claiming Child Benefit for Alex. All eligibility criteria are met.
arguments.overall_reasoning: The claimant lives in the UK and is responsible for Alex, a 6-year-old child who lives with them. Alex was temporarily in care for about 7 weeks but has since returned home. Since the care period was under 8 weeks and Alex is now back with the claimant, this does not affect eligibility. No one else is claiming Child Benefit for Alex. The claimant meets all the requirements to claim Child Benefit for Alex.

Although Alex was in care for approximately 7 weeks, this is under the 8-week limit after which Child Benefit payments would normally be affected. Additionally, Alex is now back home with the claimant. No one else is currently claiming Child Benefit for Alex. All eligibility criteria are met.

Alex was temporarily in care for about 7 weeks but has since returned home. Since the care period was under 8 weeks and Alex is now back with the claimant, this does not affect eligibility. No one else is claiming Child Benefit for Alex. The claimant meets all the requirements to claim Child Benefit for Alex.
